solution.p: We will factor 121x^{2}r^{2}-36 where a=121, b=0, and c=-36.

Difference of Squares Formula: The formula for a difference of squares is a^2-b^2 = (a+b)(a-b) Note that the polynomial 121x^{2}r^{2}-36 matches the formula's right side, and we have: \begin{aligned}[t]121x^{2}r^{2}-36\amp =\left(11xr\right)^2-\left(6\right)^2\newline \amp =\left(11xr+6\right)\mathopen{}\left(11xr-6\right)\end{aligned}
AC Method: In this method, we first calculate the product of a and c in ax^2+bx+c. For this problem, we have (121) \cdot (-36) = -4356.

The number -4356 can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways:  egin{array}{lll}1(-4356)\amp -1(4356)\amp 2(-2178)\amp -2(2178)
ewline3(-1452)\amp -3(1452)\amp 4(-1089)\amp -4(1089)
ewline6(-726)\amp -6(726)\amp 9(-484)\amp -9(484)
ewline11(-396)\amp -11(396)\amp 12(-363)\amp -12(363)
ewline18(-242)\amp -18(242)\amp 22(-198)\amp -22(198)
ewline33(-132)\amp -33(132)\amp 36(-121)\amp -36(121)
ewline44(-99)\amp -44(99)\amp 66(-66)\amp -66(66)
ewline\end{array} Looking at the corresponding sums, we want a sum of 0, and this sum happens with (-66)+66=0 So we write  egin{aligned}[t]121x^{2}r^{2}-36\amp =121x^{2}r^{2}-66xr+66xr-36
ewline \amp =11xr\mathopen{}\left(11xr-6ight)+6\mathopen{}\left(11xr-6ight)
ewline \amp =\left(11xr+6ight)\mathopen{}\left(11xr-6ight)\end{aligned}Generic Rectangles Method: First, we set up generic rectangles by putting the first term and third term in 121x^{2}r^{2}-36 into the top left and bottom right rectangles:
The first term's coefficient, 121, can be factored into the product of two numbers (where the first factor is positive) in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(121)\amp 11(11)\amp \end{array}

The third term, -36, can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(-36)\amp -1(36)\amp 2(-18)\amp -2(18)\newline3(-12)\amp -3(12)\amp 4(-9)\amp -4(9)\newline6(-6)\amp -6(6)\amp \end{array}

We put each pair into the corresponding places next to those generic rectangles, and try to match the area of those rectangles with 121x^{2}r^{2}-36 by guess-and-check. The following generic rectangles show the solution:
Dimensions of those generic rectangles in the diagram give us the solution: \begin{aligned}[t]121x^{2}r^{2}-36\amp =\left(11xr+6\right)\mathopen{}\left(11xr-6\right)\end{aligned}
